query: plan a non-overlapping CIDR address scheme for prod, dev, and test environments

metrics.content:
**Assumptions:** single-region baseline, no existing on-prem/cloud CIDRs provided, using RFC1918 `10.64.0.0/12` reserved for these environments.

| Environment | CIDR | Purpose |
|---|---:|---|
| Prod | `10.64.0.0/16` | Production workloads |
| Dev | `10.65.0.0/16` | Development workloads |
| Test | `10.66.0.0/16` | Test/QA workloads |
| Reserved | `10.67.0.0/16` - `10.79.0.0/16` | Future environments, regions, or expansion |

Suggested per-environment subnet pattern:

| Subnet | Prod | Dev | Test |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Web | `10.64.10.0/24` | `10.65.10.0/24` | `10.66.10.0/24` |
| App | `10.64.20.0/24` | `10.65.20.0/24` | `10.66.20.0/24` |
| Data | `10.64.30.0/24` | `10.65.30.0/24` | `10.66.30.0/24` |
| Management | `10.64.40.0/24` | `10.65.40.0/24` | `10.66.40.0/24` |
| Private Endpoints | `10.64.50.0/24` | `10.65.50.0/24` | `10.66.50.0/24` |
| Reserved | remaining `/24`s | remaining `/24`s | remaining `/24`s |

This keeps environments non-overlapping, easy to summarize by `/16`, and leaves substantial space for growth. Before adopting, confirm these ranges do not overlap with on-premises, VPN, partner, or existing cloud networks.

> Analysis only — verify against vendor documentation before applying.
